message:
fix(tls): handle write-blocked state correctly in TLS stream polling

The TlsStream poll_io implementation had a subtle bug where write-blocked
conditions were not properly propagated. When poll_write_tls returns
Poll::Pending but there's nothing to read, the outer loop would continue
spinning instead of yielding correctly.

Changes:
- Track write_would_block flag when write polling returns Pending
- Return Poll::Pending when both read and write paths are blocked
- Prevents busy-spinning in async contexts where writes are backpressured

This fix addresses scenarios where the underlying IO is congested and the
TLS layer needs to wait for write capacity before making progress. Without
this change, the async runtime would continually wake the task even though
no progress could be made until the socket becomes writable again.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
